raw_response_obj.cot_content:
We need to respond with a message that starts with STRATEGIC REASONING: {...}, then next. According to rules: We must include STRATEGIC REASONING only once at the very beginning of every one of our messages and not more often. Must be before any other tags. Thus the first line must be STRATEGIC REASONING: {...}.

Also must include a PROPOSAL as next. The order can be: STRATEGIC REASONING: {...} PROPOSAL: {...} ARGUMENT:{...}. All in same message? The protocol says: "You may only use the following structured formats in a message:" list. We can include multiple. We must have STRATEGIC REASONING first. We must also include an ARGUMENT as part of a message. Do we need to include AGREE or REFUSE? Not necessary. We can send PROPOSAL with ARGUMENT.

Let's produce answer. Ensure formatting correct: Each tag colon space then python set or string. For STRATEGIC REASONING: {...} the contents are a string? They said "Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action." It can be any string but must be Python set? Wait the format defined: "STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}" the inside is a python set containing 0 or more strings? Probably they want a string argument. Use {'...'} with string. Okay.

Thus output:

STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'} maybe the string inside could be "I replaced B81 with B95 and C84 ...".

Then PROPOSAL: {...} set of items.

Then ARGUMENT: {'...'}.

Make sure set syntax uses single quotes around strings, and comma separated. Ensure braces included.

Let's craft.

We need to ensure total effort: compute new set: original 9276 minus 896 + 448 + 440 = 9276 - 896 + 888 = 9276 - 8 = 9268. Yes.

Set: {'A71','C17','B15','A94','B43','A25','C53','B69','B96','A39','A65','C82','C10','A28','B16','B19','A97','C79','B95','C84','C83','C60'}

Check that all strings consistent. Also keep same case.

Now produce final message.

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. We need to respond. We can either REFUSE or make new proposal or propose to accept? We want to get set with high value. Our previous best set: we had our set with B81 instead of B95 and C84. Now opponent proposes again with B95 and C84 instead of B81. We need to reason: Our set has higher importance 11292 vs opponent's set with B95 and C84: We should calculate opponent's set importance. Let's compute quickly. Our set importance 11292. Opponent's set adds B95 (544) and C84 (536), but removes B81 (992). Also their set still has ... Let's compute difference: ours had B81 992, they have B95 544 + C84 536 = 1080, plus other items same. So they get +88. So opponent's set offers higher value by 88. But our set has higher importance? Wait 11292; opponent: 11292 + 88 = 11380. So opponent has better set. Actually B81 is more valuable at 992, but they add both B95+ C84 that sum to 1080 > 992. So they get higher importance. Also their effort? We need to check limiting: both sets have same items except swapping B81 -> B95+C84. Effort difference: B81 = 896, B95=448, C84=440 => total 888. so effort reduces by 8. So their set better both value and effort. So they are offering a better set. We could accept. But we need to AGREE to the opponent's proposal? If we AGREE, we get that set, giving us 11380. That's even higher than our own set, but we accept. If we refuse, we may risk opponent refusing our set? We can attempt to make a better set? But we don't know their importances. Usually we want to maximize our own, so giving them a better set reduces our value, but might get them to stay. However we can't negotiate further after acceptance. But we could propose something new. But if we propose something new that includes all our items plus maybe 2 new high-value? But constraint limit.

We can see we are not sure of opponent's values, but our own set is less than opponent's. Accepting gives us higher importance too. But we might want to propose a slightly improved set adding some other items that we think benefit both? But we could just accept: AGREE to their set. But that seems too easy. Maybe we propose a superset that includes both B81 and B95+C84? But limit might exceed. Let's approximate efforts: our set had effort 9276. The opponent's set replaces B81 (896) with B95(448)+C84(440)=888, difference -8, so effort 9268. So limit 9322 holds.

Could we add other items like B34 or C83? Wait C83 already in. B34? not in set. B34 effort 821, importance 917. That would exceed limit maybe? Current 9268 + 821 = 10089 > limit. So no.

Our set is optimum maybe. So accept opponent's set. We must use AGREE. According to protocol, we must provide ARGUMENT also, maybe.

We need to follow format: include STRATEGIC REASONING only once at beginning. That's fine. Provide AGREE and ARGUMENT. No REFUSE. So we will output.
